excerpt: Official U.S. after-action summary of the assembled Manila defenders, mostly naval but including army personnel and others organized into provisional combat units.
transform_note: Used as the starting Japanese defender stock for the military person-month model.
confidence_note: Rounded official estimate; good for theater-scale defender presence.

excerpt: Huff reports the prewar Manila population peak as the lowest credible citywide anchor in this bundle.
transform_note: Used as the conservative low-start stock for civilian person-month modeling.
confidence_note: Useful as a floor, but probably below actual battle-start population because of wartime refugee inflows.

excerpt: Japanese-era archival estimate showing Manila had already greatly exceeded its prewar population during occupation.
transform_note: Used as the best-start stock in the civilian person-month model because it is a direct wartime city estimate and is more conservative than the 1944 peak.
confidence_note: A solid archival stock figure, but not a direct January-February 1945 count.

excerpt: Huff prefers this mayoral/neighborhood-based wartime estimate over noisier higher claims such as 1.3 million or 1.5 million.
transform_note: Used as the high-start stock in the civilian person-month model.
confidence_note: Still an indirect prebattle anchor, but the best high-side city-population stock in the reviewed material.

excerpt: Civil Affairs Unit No. 20 census anchor for the remaining city population during/just after the battle month.
transform_note: Used as the end-of-episode lower anchor in the civilian person-month model.
confidence_note: Precise timing and coverage inside February are unclear, so this claim drives high denominator uncertainty.

excerpt: Japanese body count reported for the battle.
transform_note: Summed with U.S. killed to build the core military direct-death estimate.
confidence_note: Strong as an official body-count style figure, though body counts can still involve some reporting error.

excerpt: U.S. killed reported for the city battle.
transform_note: Summed with Japanese killed for the military direct-death estimate.
confidence_note: Good official-style figure, though some later secondary works give a slightly different U.S. total.

excerpt: U.S. wounded reported for the battle.
transform_note: Retained as an auxiliary injury series only; not converted into deaths and not treated as total military injuries for all sides.
confidence_note: Partial military injury series because equivalent Japanese/Filipino wound totals were not recovered.

excerpt: Postwar Philippine-government estimate for non-combatant civilians killed during the battle.
transform_note: Used as the best point anchor for the civilian direct-death estimate, but not accepted uncritically.
confidence_note: Highly influential figure, but its documentary pedigree is weak and contested.

excerpt: Meixsel quotes an April 1945 OSS interview reporting that many Filipinos placed the civilian death total at 30,000.
transform_note: Used as the low scenario anchor for civilian direct deaths, while recognizing likely incompleteness so soon after the battle.
confidence_note: Important as evidence of contemporaneous lower estimates, but likely incomplete because body recovery and full access to massacre sites were still evolving.

excerpt: General Beightler's manuscript comment opposing Smith's 100,000 figure.
transform_note: Not used as the low estimate because it is an undocumented command comment that appears strongly interested and implausibly low relative to other evidence.
confidence_note: Useful as disagreement evidence, weak as a standalone mortality estimate.

excerpt: Meixsel relays Hartendorp's report of an undertaker's burial count for civilians buried under an army contract, explicitly excluding other bodies.
transform_note: Used only as a floor fragment, not as a total-death estimate.
confidence_note: Partial subset only; explicitly excludes bodies cremated where found and other unburied victims.

excerpt: Meixsel relays Hartendorp's note that later reports suggested up to ten thousand civilians murdered in Intramuros alone.
transform_note: Used as evidence that very high civilian losses were plausible in major subareas even if the citywide total remains disputed.
confidence_note: Subset claim for one district, not a citywide total.

excerpt: Nakano summarizes military historians who argue that U.S. shelling may explain as much as roughly two-fifths of civilian deaths.
transform_note: Used only for mechanism-of-death discussion, not to generate separate citywide shelling and massacre death totals.
confidence_note: Upper-bound attribution share, not a cleanly separated numeric death series.

excerpt: Nakano's city-force cross-check for the Manila Defense Forces remaining in the city.
transform_note: Used as a cross-check against the XIV Corps figure of 18,400 defenders.
confidence_note: Rounded secondary synthesis; supportive rather than primary for the defender denominator.
